title: Heatwave alert in Wanaparthy: Temperatures to reach upto 45°C - The Hans India
full_text:
Heatwave alert in Wanaparthy: Temperatures to reach upto 45°C
In view of the rise in temperatures, District Collector Adarsh Surabhi has issued a public advisory urging residents to remain vigilant against severe heatwave conditions expected over the next week.
Chairing a review meeting with officials from all district departments at the Collectorate conference hall on MOnday, the Collector highlighted that temperatures are likely to soar up to 45°C, accompanied by strong heatwaves. He directed officials to take proactive measures to prevent heatstroke cases and ensure public safety.
Emphasizsng preparedness, the Collector instructed authorities to establish cooling centers across all municipalities and mandal headquarters to provide relief to the public. He also called for widespread awareness campaigns to educate citizens about the risks of heatwaves and the precautions they must adopt.
To mitigate the effects of extreme heat, arrangements are being made to supply drinking water at key public locations. The Collector advised citizens to carry water bottles whenever they step outdoors and avoid unnecessary exposure to the sun.
Special focus was placed on ensuring basic amenities across institutions. All government offices have been directed to provide adequate drinking water facilities.
Additionally, Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) will be made available in government hospitals, schools, colleges, and welfare hostels to safeguard students and patients from dehydration.
For labourers working under employment guarantee schemes, officials were instructed to ensure the availability of cool drinking water and shaded rest areas at worksites. The Collector issued a stern warning that strict action would be taken against negligence in providing these essential facilities.
Farmers were also advised to refrain from agricultural activities during peak afternoon hours to avoid heat-related illnesses. Officials from Mission Bhagiratha have been tasked with maintaining uninterrupted drinking water supply across the district, ensuring timely distribution during the summer season.
On the occasion, the Collector, along with senior officials, unveiled an awareness poster prepared by the District Medical and Health Department outlining precautionary measures to combat heatwaves.
The meeting was attended by Additional Collector (Revenue) Vinod Kumar, ZP CEO Yadaiah, DSP Balaji Naik, DTO Manasa, DRDO Umadevi, CPO Harikrishna, and other district officials.
